clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall perform the event photography services described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] at [EVENT_LOCATION] on [EVENT_DATE], for an estimated duration of [ESTIMATED_HOURS] hours.
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [EVENT_DATE] at [EVENT_LOCATION], subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [HOURLY_RATE] per hour, for an estimated total of [TOTAL_VALUE] based on [ESTIMATED_HOURS] hours, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD].
If the Services extend beyond [ESTIMATED_HOURS] hours at the Client's request, the Client shall pay [OVERTIME_RATE] per additional hour.

The Client shall be responsible for informing attendees of [EVENT_LOCATION] that photography will take place at the event, including through signage or announcement, and for handling any attendee's request to be excluded from photography.

Upon full payment, the Client receives a license to use the Deliverables for its own promotional and internal purposes related to the event described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED]. The Service Provider retains copyright in the Deliverables.

If the Client cancels the Services more than [CANCELLATION_NOTICE_PERIOD_DAYS] days before [EVENT_DATE], the Client shall forfeit [CANCELLATION_FEE_PERCENT] of [TOTAL_VALUE]. If the Client cancels within [CANCELLATION_NOTICE_PERIOD_DAYS] days of [EVENT_DATE], the Client shall forfeit the full amount of [TOTAL_VALUE].

The Client may request to reschedule [EVENT_DATE] by giving at least [RESCHEDULING_NOTICE_PERIOD_DAYS] days' written notice, subject to the Service Provider's availability. The Service Provider may charge a rescheduling fee of [RESCHEDULING_FEE_AMOUNT] where availability requires reallocation of resources.

The Client may grant the Service Provider a non-exclusive right to display the Deliverables for the Service Provider's professional portfolio and promotional purposes. This right does not apply where the Confidentiality Clause designates the Deliverables as confidential, or where [PORTFOLIO_AUTHORIZATION_DECLINED] indicates the Client has declined.
Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from this Agreement.
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — hourly pricing paired with an explicit overtime rate, closing the most common dispute for time-based event coverage. Duplication: Image Rights (attendee notice mechanism) vs Wedding's Image Rights (individual guest consent) intentionally differ in mechanism — reflecting genuinely distinct legal risk (mass-attendee vs. small-guest-list events) rather than unjustified divergence. Loophole closed: added explicit overtime clause, absent from Wedding's flat-fee model, appropriate to this Contract Type's hourly structure. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Attendee Notice Clause shifts practical consent-management burden to Client, who controls the venue/event, rather than the photographer who cannot control attendee flow. Complexity: Cancellation/Rescheduling reused identically from Wedding pattern per consistency rule, since date-fixed risk profile is the same. Enforceability: overtime explicitly conditioned on "Client's request," preventing unilateral overtime billing by photographer. Attack: Photographer works well beyond estimated hours without prior confirmation, then bills overtime — mitigated: overtime clause requires it be "at the Client's request." Attack: An attendee objects to being photographed and sues the photographer directly — mitigated: Image Rights Clause contractually assigns attendee-notice and exclusion-handling responsibility to the Client/organizer. Attack: Client cancels two days before a corporate event — mitigated by Cancellation Clause reused from Wedding's tiered-forfeiture pattern. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
